title: Changes in reading habits
subtitle: What are the implications of the way we read today?

sentences.en: Look around on your next plane trip. The iPad is the new pacifier for babies and toddlers.
sentences.zh: 下次坐飞机时环顾四周吧。iPad已成了婴幼儿新的“安抚奶嘴”。

grammar.type: 祈使句 + 主系表
grammar.note: Look around on your next plane trip 为祈使句；The iPad is the new pacifier for babies and toddlers 为主系表；pacifier 意为 “安抚奶嘴”，此处为比喻；toddler 意为 “学步的幼儿”。

sentences.en: Younger school-aged children read stories on smartphones; older kids don’t read at all, but hunch over video games.
sentences.zh: 年龄较小的学龄儿童在智能手机上读故事；年龄较大的孩子则根本不读书，而是弓着身子玩电子游戏。

grammar.type: 分号并列 + but 转折
grammar.note: 分号连接两个分句；older kids don't read at all, but hunch over video games 中 but 表转折，hunch over 意为 “弓身伏在……上”；school-aged 意为 “学龄的”；not... at all 意为 “根本不”。

sentences.en: Parents and other passengers read on tablets or skim a flotilla of email and news feeds.
sentences.zh: 父母和其他乘客则在平板电脑上阅读，或者浏览大量的电子邮件和新闻推送。

grammar.type: or 并列谓语
grammar.note: read on tablets or skim a flotilla of... 为 or 连接的并列谓语；skim 意为 “略读、浏览”；a flotilla of 原指 “一支小型船队”，此处比喻 “大量的”；news feed 意为 “新闻推送”。

sentences.en: Unbeknown to most of us, an invisible, game-changing transformation links everyone in this picture: the neuronal circuit that underlies the brain’s ability to read is subtly, rapidly changing and this has implications for everyone from the pre-reading toddler to the expert adult.
sentences.zh: 在我们大多数人未曾察觉之际，一场无形而颠覆性的变革把这幅画面中的每个人联系了起来：支撑大脑阅读能力的神经回路正在悄然而迅速地发生变化，这对每个人——从尚不识字的幼儿到熟练的成年人——都有影响。

grammar.type: 独立主格状语 + 冒号 + that 定语从句
grammar.note: Unbeknown to most of us 为形容词短语作状语，意为 “我们大多数人不知道的是”；冒号后为对 transformation 的说明；the neuronal circuit that underlies... 含 that 定语从句；game-changing 意为 “颠覆性的”，neuronal circuit 意为 “神经回路”。

sentences.en: As work in neurosciences indicates, the acquisition of literacy necessitated a new circuit in our species’ brain more than 6,000 years ago.
sentences.zh: 正如神经科学研究所表明的那样，六千多年前，识字能力的获得使我们这个物种的大脑必须形成一种新的回路。

grammar.type: As 非限制性从句
grammar.note: As work in neurosciences indicates 为 as 引导的非限制性定语从句（评注）；主句 the acquisition of literacy necessitated a new circuit；necessitate 意为 “使成为必需”；the acquisition of literacy 意为 “识字能力的获得”。

sentences.en: That circuit evolved from a very simple mechanism for decoding basic information, like the number of goats in one’s herd, to the present, highly elaborated reading brain.
sentences.zh: 这一回路从一种极为简单的、用于解码基本信息（比如自家羊群里羊的数量）的机制，演化为如今高度精细化的阅读大脑。

grammar.type: evolve from ... to ...
grammar.note: evolve from A to B 意为 “从 A 演化为 B”；for decoding basic information 为介词短语作定语修饰 mechanism；like the number of goats in one's herd 为举例插入；highly elaborated 意为 “高度精细化的”；herd 意为 “畜群”。

sentences.en: My research depicts how the present reading brain enables the development of some of our most important intellectual and affective processes: internalized knowledge, analogical reasoning, and inference; perspective-taking and empathy; critical analysis and the generation of insight.
sentences.zh: 我的研究描绘了当今的阅读大脑如何促成我们一些最重要的智力和情感过程的发展：内化的知识、类比推理和推断；换位思考与共情；批判性分析以及洞见的产生。

grammar.type: how 宾语从句 + 冒号列举
grammar.note: depicts how... 为 how 引导的宾语从句；enable the development of 意为 “促成……的发展”；冒号后为三组并列列举，用分号分隔；analogical reasoning 意为 “类比推理”，perspective-taking 意为 “换位思考”，affective 意为 “情感的”。

sentences.en: Research surfacing in many parts of the world now cautions that each of these essential ‘deep reading’ processes may be under threat as we move into digital-based modes of reading.
sentences.zh: 如今在世界许多地方涌现的研究都发出警告：随着我们步入以数字为基础的阅读方式，这些至关重要的“深度阅读”过程都可能面临威胁。

grammar.type: 现在分词定语 + that 宾语从句 + as 时间从句
grammar.note: surfacing in many parts of the world 为现在分词短语作定语修饰 Research；cautions that... 为宾语从句；as we move into digital-based modes of reading 为 as 时间状语从句；surface 作动词意为 “浮现、出现”，be under threat 意为 “受到威胁”。

sentences.en: This is not a simple, binary issue of print versus digital reading and technological innovation.
sentences.zh: 这并非一个关于纸质阅读与数字阅读、以及技术创新的简单二元问题。

grammar.type: 主系表
grammar.note: This is not a simple, binary issue of... 为主系表结构；binary 意为 “二元的、非此即彼的”；print versus digital reading 意为 “纸质对数字阅读”；versus 意为 “对、相对于”。

sentences.en: As MIT scholar Sherry Turkle has written, we do not err as a society when we innovate but when we ignore what we disrupt or diminish while innovating.
sentences.zh: 正如麻省理工学院学者雪莉·特克尔所写，作为一个社会，我们的失误不在于创新之时，而在于创新的同时忽视了自己所破坏或削弱的东西之时。

grammar.type: As 从句 + not ... but ... + what 宾语从句
grammar.note: As MIT scholar Sherry Turkle has written 为 as 引导的评注从句；not... when we innovate but when we ignore... 为 not...but... 连接两个 when 从句；what we disrupt or diminish 为宾语从句；while innovating 为省略的时间状语；err 意为 “犯错”。

sentences.en: In this hinge moment between print and digital cultures, society needs to confront what is diminishing in the expert reading circuit, what our children and older students are not developing, and what we can do about it.
sentences.zh: 在这个介于纸质文化与数字文化之间的转折时刻，社会需要正视：熟练阅读回路中正在削弱的是什么，我们的孩子和年长学生尚未发展出什么，以及我们对此能做些什么。

grammar.type: 三个 what 宾语从句并列
grammar.note: In this hinge moment between... 为时间状语，hinge moment 意为 “转折时刻”；confront 后接三个并列的 what 宾语从句；confront 意为 “正视、面对”；expert reading circuit 意为 “熟练阅读回路”。

sentences.en: We know from research that the reading circuit is not given to human beings through a genetic blueprint like vision or language; it needs an environment to develop.
sentences.zh: 我们从研究中得知，阅读回路并不像视觉或语言那样，是通过某种基因蓝图赋予人类的；它需要一个环境才能发展。

grammar.type: that 宾语从句 + 分号并列
grammar.note: know from research that... 为宾语从句；is not given to human beings through a genetic blueprint 为被动；like vision or language 为举例；分号后 it needs an environment to develop 为并列分句；genetic blueprint 意为 “基因蓝图”。

sentences.en: Further, it will adapt to that environment’s requirements – from different writing systems to the characteristics of whatever medium is used.
sentences.zh: 此外，它会适应那个环境的种种要求——从不同的书写系统到所用媒介的各种特性。

grammar.type: adapt to + from ... to ...
grammar.note: adapt to that environment's requirements 意为 “适应那个环境的要求”；破折号后 from different writing systems to the characteristics of whatever medium is used 为 from...to... 结构；whatever medium is used 为 whatever 引导的名词性从句；characteristic 意为 “特性”。

sentences.en: If the dominant medium advantages processes that are fast, multi-task oriented and well-suited for large volumes of information, like the current digital medium, so will the reading circuit.
sentences.zh: 如果占主导地位的媒介偏向那些快速、面向多任务、且适合处理大量信息的过程——正如当前的数字媒介那样——那么阅读回路也会随之如此。

grammar.type: if 条件从句 + so 倒装
grammar.note: If the dominant medium advantages processes that are... 为条件从句，advantage 此处作动词意为 “有利于”，含 that 定语从句；so will the reading circuit 为 so + 助动词 + 主语的倒装，表 “……也会如此”；multi-task oriented 意为 “面向多任务的”，well-suited for 意为 “很适合”。

sentences.en: As UCLA psychologist Patricia Greenfield writes, the result is that less attention and time will be allocated to slower, time-demanding deep reading processes.
sentences.zh: 正如加州大学洛杉矶分校心理学家帕特里夏·格林菲尔德所写，其结果是，分配给较慢、耗时的深度阅读过程的注意力和时间将会减少。

grammar.type: As 从句 + that 表语从句 + 被动
grammar.note: As UCLA psychologist Patricia Greenfield writes 为评注从句；the result is that... 为表语从句；less attention and time will be allocated to... 为被动；time-demanding 意为 “耗时的”，allocate to 意为 “分配给”。

sentences.en: Increasing reports from educators and from researchers in psychology and the humanities bear this out.
sentences.zh: 来自教育工作者以及心理学和人文学科研究者的越来越多的报告都证实了这一点。

grammar.type: 简单句
grammar.note: 主语 Increasing reports from educators and from researchers in psychology and the humanities；谓语 bear this out；bear out 意为 “证实”；the humanities 意为 “人文学科”；educator 意为 “教育工作者”。

sentences.en: English literature scholar and teacher Mark Edmundson describes how many college students actively avoid the classic literature of the 19th and 20th centuries in favour of something simpler as they no longer have the patience to read longer, denser, more difficult texts.
sentences.zh: 英语文学学者兼教师马克·埃德蒙森描述了许多大学生如何主动回避19和20世纪的经典文学、转而选择更简单的读物，因为他们不再有耐心去阅读更长、更密集、更艰深的文本。

grammar.type: how 宾语从句 + in favour of + as 原因从句
grammar.note: describes how many college students actively avoid... 为 how 宾语从句；in favour of something simpler 意为 “转而青睐更简单的东西”；as they no longer have the patience to read... 为原因状语从句；denser 意为 “更密集的”，patience 意为 “耐心”。

sentences.en: We should be less concerned with students’ ‘cognitive impatience’, however, than by what may underlie it: the potential inability of large numbers of students to read with a level of critical analysis sufficient to comprehend the complexity of thought and argument found in more demanding texts.
sentences.zh: 然而，与其担忧学生的“认知急躁”，我们更应关注其背后可能潜藏的东西：大量学生可能无法以足够的批判性分析水平去阅读，从而难以理解更具挑战性文本中思想和论证的复杂性。

grammar.type: less ... than ... + 冒号 + 过去分词定语
grammar.note: be less concerned with... than by what may underlie it 为比较结构，however 为插入语；冒号后为对 what may underlie it 的说明；sufficient to comprehend... 为形容词短语作定语；found in more demanding texts 为过去分词定语；cognitive impatience 意为 “认知急躁”。

sentences.en: Multiple studies show that digital screen use may be causing a variety of troubling downstream effects on reading comprehension in older high school and college students.
sentences.zh: 多项研究表明，使用数字屏幕可能正在对年龄较大的高中生和大学生的阅读理解产生各种令人担忧的连锁后果。

grammar.type: that 宾语从句
grammar.note: Multiple studies show that... 为宾语从句；may be causing 为现在进行时的推测；a variety of troubling downstream effects 意为 “各种令人担忧的连锁后果”；downstream 意为 “下游的、后续的”，reading comprehension 意为 “阅读理解”。

sentences.en: In Stavanger, Norway, psychologist Anne Mangen and her colleagues studied how high school students comprehend the same material in different mediums.
sentences.zh: 在挪威的斯塔万格，心理学家安妮·曼根及其同事研究了高中生在不同媒介中对同一材料的理解情况。

grammar.type: how 宾语从句
grammar.note: In Stavanger, Norway 为地点状语；studied how high school students comprehend the same material 为 how 宾语从句；in different mediums 为方式状语；comprehend 意为 “理解”，material 意为 “材料”。

sentences.en: Mangen’s group asked subjects questions about a short story whose plot had universal student appeal; half of the students read the story on a tablet, the other half in paperback.
sentences.zh: 曼根的团队就一篇情节对所有学生都有普遍吸引力的短篇小说向受试者提问；一半学生在平板电脑上阅读，另一半则读纸质平装本。

grammar.type: whose 定语从句 + 分号并列
grammar.note: asked subjects questions about a short story 为主句；whose plot had universal student appeal 为 whose 定语从句修饰 story；分号后 half... the other half... 为并列分句；subject 此处意为 “受试者”，paperback 意为 “平装本”。

sentences.en: Results indicated that students who read on print were superior in their comprehension to screen-reading peers, particularly in their ability to sequence detail and reconstruct the plot in chronological order.
sentences.zh: 结果表明，纸质阅读的学生在理解方面优于屏幕阅读的同龄人，尤其是在按时间顺序排列细节和重建情节的能力上。

grammar.type: that 宾语从句 + who 定语从句 + be superior to
grammar.note: indicated that... 为宾语从句；who read on print 为定语从句；be superior to 意为 “优于”；particularly in their ability to sequence detail and reconstruct the plot 为补充状语；in chronological order 意为 “按时间顺序”，sequence 作动词意为 “按顺序排列”。

sentences.en: Ziming Liu from San Jose State University has conducted a series of studies which indicate that the ‘new norm’ in reading is skimming, involving word-spotting and browsing through the text.
sentences.zh: 圣何塞州立大学的刘子明进行了一系列研究，这些研究表明，阅读中的“新常态”是略读，即在文本中寻找关键词并浏览。

grammar.type: which 定语从句 + that 宾语从句 + 现在分词状语
grammar.note: has conducted a series of studies which indicate that... 含 which 定语从句，其后接 that 宾语从句；involving word-spotting and browsing through the text 为现在分词作伴随状语；new norm 意为 “新常态”，word-spotting 意为 “找词、扫词”，browse 意为 “浏览”。

sentences.en: Many readers now use a pattern when reading in which they sample the first line and then word-spot through the rest of the text.
sentences.zh: 如今许多读者在阅读时采用一种模式：先浏览第一行，然后在文本的其余部分扫读关键词。

grammar.type: in which 定语从句
grammar.note: use a pattern when reading 为主句，when reading 为省略的时间状语；in which they sample the first line and then word-spot... 为 in which 定语从句修饰 pattern，含并列谓语；sample 此处意为 “抽看、浏览”；the rest of 意为 “……的其余部分”。

sentences.en: When the reading brain skims like this, it reduces time allocated to deep reading processes.
sentences.zh: 当阅读大脑像这样略读时，它会减少分配给深度阅读过程的时间。

grammar.type: When 时间从句 + 过去分词定语
grammar.note: When the reading brain skims like this 为时间状语从句；主句 it reduces time；allocated to deep reading processes 为过去分词短语作定语修饰 time；skim 意为 “略读”，reduce 意为 “减少”。

sentences.en: In other words, we don’t have time to grasp complexity, to understand another’s feelings, to perceive beauty, and to create thoughts of the reader’s own.
sentences.zh: 换句话说，我们没有时间去把握复杂性、去理解他人的感受、去感知美、去形成读者自己的思想。

grammar.type: 四个并列不定式
grammar.note: In other words 意为 “换句话说”；have time 后接四个并列不定式 to grasp... to understand... to perceive... and to create...；grasp 意为 “把握、领会”，perceive 意为 “感知”。

sentences.en: The possibility that critical analysis, empathy and other deep reading processes could become the unintended ‘collateral damage’ of our digital culture is not a straightforward binary issue about print versus digital reading.
sentences.zh: 批判性分析、共情以及其他深度阅读过程有可能成为我们数字文化中意料之外的“附带损害”，但这并非一个关于纸质与数字阅读的简单二元问题。

grammar.type: that 同位语从句
grammar.note: The possibility that... 中 that 引导同位语从句说明 possibility；主句谓语 is not a straightforward binary issue；unintended 意为 “意料之外的”，collateral damage 意为 “附带损害”，straightforward 意为 “简单直接的”。

sentences.en: It is about how we all have begun to read on various mediums and how that changes not only what we read, but also the purposes for which we read.
sentences.zh: 它关乎我们所有人如何开始在各种媒介上阅读，以及这如何不仅改变了我们读什么，也改变了我们为何而读。

grammar.type: 两个 how 宾语从句 + not only ... but also
grammar.note: It is about how... and how... 为两个并列 how 宾语从句；that changes not only what we read, but also the purposes for which we read 中 not only...but also... 连接两个宾语，for which we read 为定语从句修饰 purposes；various 意为 “各种的”。

sentences.en: Nor is it only about the young. The subtle atrophy of critical analysis and empathy affects us all equally.
sentences.zh: 它也并不只关乎年轻人。批判性分析与共情能力的悄然萎缩，同等地影响着我们所有人。

grammar.type: Nor 倒装
grammar.note: Nor is it only about the young 为 Nor 引导的部分倒装，表 “也不……”；The subtle atrophy of critical analysis and empathy 为下一句主语；atrophy 意为 “萎缩”，subtle 意为 “微妙的、不易察觉的”。

sentences.en: It affects our ability to navigate a constant bombardment of information. It incentivizes a retreat to the most familiar stores of unchecked information, which require and receive no analysis, leaving us susceptible to false information and irrational ideas.
sentences.zh: 它影响着我们在信息的持续轰炸中辨明方向的能力。它促使我们退回到最熟悉的、未经核实的信息来源，这些信息既不需要也得不到任何分析，从而使我们容易受到虚假信息和非理性观念的影响。

grammar.type: which 非限制性定语 + 现在分词状语
grammar.note: 两句并列；navigate a constant bombardment of information 意为 “在信息的持续轰炸中辨明方向”；incentivize a retreat to 意为 “激励/促使退回到”；which require and receive no analysis 为非限制性定语从句；leaving us susceptible to... 为现在分词作结果状语；be susceptible to 意为 “易受……影响”。

sentences.en: There’s an old rule in neuroscience that does not alter with age: use it or lose it.
sentences.zh: 神经科学中有一条不随年龄改变的古老法则：用进废退。

grammar.type: that 定语从句 + 冒号
grammar.note: There's an old rule in neuroscience that does not alter with age 含 that 定语从句修饰 rule；冒号后 use it or lose it 为对该法则的具体表述；alter with 意为 “随……而改变”。

sentences.en: It is a very hopeful principle when applied to critical thought in the reading brain because it implies choice.
sentences.zh: 当把它应用于阅读大脑中的批判性思维时，这是一条极具希望的原则，因为它意味着选择。

grammar.type: when 省略从句 + because 原因
grammar.note: when applied to critical thought 为省略主语和 be 的时间从句（= when it is applied to...）；主句 It is a very hopeful principle；because it implies choice 为原因从句；imply 意为 “暗示、意味着”，principle 意为 “原则”。

sentences.en: The story of the changing reading brain is hardly finished. We possess both the science and the technology to identify and redress the changes in how we read before they become entrenched.
sentences.zh: 关于不断变化的阅读大脑的故事远未结束。我们同时拥有科学和技术，能够在这些阅读方式的变化根深蒂固之前识别并纠正它们。

grammar.type: hardly + 不定式定语 + before 从句
grammar.note: is hardly finished 意为 “远未结束”，hardly 表否定；possess both the science and the technology to identify and redress... 含并列不定式定语；before they become entrenched 为时间状语从句；redress 意为 “纠正”，entrenched 意为 “根深蒂固的”。

sentences.en: If we work to understand exactly what we will lose, alongside the extraordinary new capacities that the digital world has brought us, there is as much reason for excitement as caution.
sentences.zh: 如果我们努力弄清楚——在数字世界带给我们非凡新能力的同时——我们究竟会失去什么，那么我们既有理由感到兴奋，也同样有理由保持警惕。

grammar.type: if 条件从句 + that 定语从句 + as much ... as ...
grammar.note: If we work to understand exactly what we will lose 为条件从句，含 what 宾语从句；alongside the extraordinary new capacities that... 为介词短语加 that 定语从句；主句 there is as much reason for excitement as caution 为 as...as 同级比较；alongside 意为 “与……一起”，caution 意为 “警惕”。

questions.title: Questions 14–17
instructions: Choose the correct letter, A, B, C or D.
Write the correct letter in boxes 14–17 on your answer sheet.

items.prompt:
What is the writer’s main point in the first paragraph?
A Our use of technology is having a hidden effect on us.
B Technology can be used to help youngsters to read.
C Travellers should be encouraged to use technology on planes.
D Playing games is a more popular use of technology than reading.

What main point does Sherry Turkle make about innovation?
A Technological innovation has led to a reduction in print reading.
B We should pay attention to what might be lost when innovation occurs.
C We should encourage more young people to become involved in innovation.
D There is a difference between developing products and developing ideas.

What point is the writer making in the fourth paragraph?
A Humans have an inborn ability to read and write.
B Reading can be done using many different mediums.
C Writing systems make unexpected demands on the brain.
D Some brain circuits adjust to whatever is required of them.

According to Mark Edmundson, the attitude of college students
A has changed the way he teaches.
B has influenced what they select to read.
C does not worry him as much as it does others.
D does not match the views of the general public.

questions.title: Questions 18–22
instructions: Complete the summary using the list of words, A–H, below.
Write the correct letter, A–H, in boxes 18–22 on your answer sheet.
Word list: A fast  B isolated  C emotional  D worrying  E many  F hard  G combined  H thorough

items.prompt: There have been many studies on digital screen use, showing some 18 ______ trends.
Her team then used a question-and-answer technique to find out how 19 ______ each group’s understanding of the plot was.
The findings showed a clear pattern ... with those who read screens finding the order of information 20 ______ to recall.
Studies by Ziming Liu show that students are tending to read 21 ______ words and phrases in a text to save time.
This approach, she says, gives the reader a superficial understanding of the 22 ______ content of material, leaving no time for thought.

questions.title: Questions 23–26
instructions: Do the following statements agree with the views of the writer in Reading Passage 2?
In boxes 23–26 on your answer sheet, write
YES if the statement agrees with the views of the writer
NO if the statement contradicts the views of the writer
NOT GIVEN if it is impossible to say what the writer thinks about this

items.prompt: The medium we use to read can affect our choice of reading content.
Some age groups are more likely to lose their complex reading skills than others.
False information has become more widespread in today’s digital era.
We still have opportunities to rectify the problems that technology is presenting.
